description: A substance used in treating disease or illness; what heals the body. 'Nepha' names what restores—the treatment that returns health.
sound_symbolism: The word nurtures healing. 'Ne' nestles with internal care, medicine entering. Then 'ha' /ɸa/ breathes with healing breath, restoration beginning.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'nepha' (the medicine). General term for healing substances. Related to 'heloa' (remedy), 'thelai' (balm), 'wenu' (tonic).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Medicine for suffering—addressing the root cause, not just symptoms, true healing.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Medicine from nature—plants, minerals, preparations passed through generations of healers.
